title: Decision Analysis: Accelerate PRD Pipeline from Sprint 14-15 to Sprint 10-11
decision_question: Should we accelerate the PRD generation pipeline by 4 sprints to maintain competitive lead after CompetitorAlpha launched AI PRD generation?

context.stakes: CompetitorAlpha launched AI-powered PRD generation, directly overlapping with ProductOS V10 core capability. Losing 4-6 week differentiation window risks competitor capturing market awareness before our launch.
constraints: Engineering team is already allocated at 100% capacity for current sprint scope
Developer handoff pack depends on PRD pipeline — delaying PRD delays downstream stories and API contracts
Gold standard validation must not be compromised by acceleration

options.name: Accelerate PRD Pipeline (4 sprints)
options.description: Move PRD generation from Sprint 14-15 to Sprint 10-11. Parallelize with remaining Discovery work. Accept slightly reduced prototype depth in earlier sprints.
options.expected_outcome: PRD generation ships within 6-8 weeks, maintaining competitive lead. Some prototype polish deferred to post-launch.
options.cost_estimate: 2 engineering weeks redirected from prototype polish

options.name: Partial Acceleration (2 sprints)
options.description: Move PRD pipeline to Sprint 12-13. Ship MVP PRD generation (core schema + basic output) while maintaining current prototype timeline.
options.expected_outcome: Competitive gap narrows but we ship something before competitor's v2. Partial feature reduces risk of rushed delivery.

options.name: Maintain Current Timeline
options.description: Keep PRD pipeline at Sprint 14-15. Invest current engineering capacity in prototype polish. Differentiate on prototype quality rather than PRD speed.
options.expected_outcome: Highest quality output at launch. But competitor may capture PRD generation mindshare before we ship.

recommendation.rationale: Option A scores highest on weighted criteria (755 vs 700 for B, 625 for C). The competitive urgency from CompetitorAlpha's launch justifies the medium risk. Time-to-ship and competitive differentiation are the highest-weighted criteria, and Option A leads both.
key_trade_offs: Accelerating PRD pipeline trades 2 weeks of prototype polish for 4 sprints of competitive lead time
Engineering team will need to context-switch more — capacity impact is the weakest score
Output quality target drops from v1_candidate to high for PRD MVP
recommendation.what_is_lost: Prototype polish in Discovery sprints — interactive elements (micro-interactions, animations) deferred to post-launch iteration. PRD MVP ships with medium fidelity initially.

assumptions_tested.assumption: CompetitorAlpha will not ship PRD generation v2 within 8 weeks
assumptions_tested.flips_decision_at: Competitor ships v2 within 4 weeks — then Option C becomes preferable to Option A

assumptions_tested.assumption: Engineering team can absorb 2 sprints of capacity reallocation without burnout
assumptions_tested.flips_decision_at: If team capacity is actually 110% (over-allocated by 10%), Option B becomes preferable — acceleration risk too high

assumptions_tested.assumption: Gold standard validation can be parallelized with PRD pipeline work
assumptions_tested.flips_decision_at: Does not flip — even at 3 sprints for validation, Option A still leads

critical_assumptions: Engineering team capacity is the most sensitive assumption — if team is over-allocated, acceleration adds burnout risk and quality drops
Competitor v2 timeline is moderately important — monitor competitor release cadence closely
sensitivity_analysis.conclusion: Decision is moderately sensitive to engineering capacity assumptions and competitor velocity. Recommendation: proceed with Option A but implement capacity safeguards — add 1 week buffer and clear kill switch if team shows burnout signals.
sensitivity_analysis.decision_holds_within_range: Decision holds unless: engineering capacity is >110% allocated, OR competitor ships v2 within 4 weeks, OR gold standard validation takes more than 2 sprints longer than estimated

failure_scenarios.scenario: PRD pipeline is rushed, ships with shallow output that PMs perceive as generic AI-generated content. Reviews are negative because quality is below what PMs expect from a tool that claims world-class PM superpowers.
leading_indicators: Beta testers report PRD outputs needing >40% rewrite
Design reviewer flags 3+ gold standard checklist failures
PM feedback mentions 'feels like ChatGPT output'

failure_scenarios.scenario: Engineering team burns out from context-switching between PRD acceleration and existing sprint commitments. Sprint velocity drops 30% across all tracks, not just PRD.
leading_indicators: 2+ engineers report feeling stretched in standups

failure_scenarios.scenario: CompetitorAlpha ships v2 of PRD generation within 4 weeks, making our accelerated timeline insufficient to capture differentiation. We rushed for nothing.
leading_indicators: Their hiring signals suggest accelerated roadmap
They announce funding round that enables faster execution

early_warning_indicators: PM beta feedback quality scores dropping below 4/5
Gold standard checklist pass rate dropping below 80%
reversal_triggers: Kill acceleration if: 2 consecutive sprints show velocity decline >20%, OR PM feedback scores average <3.5/5, OR competitor ships v2 within Sprint 9

reversibility_scoring.cost_to_reverse: 1 sprint of engineering time to re-sequence work back to original timeline
reversibility_scoring.time_to_reverse: 2 weeks to unwind acceleration and restore original schedule
reversibility_scoring.irreversible_after: Sprint 11 — once PRD pipeline code is shipped, reverting means deprecating a shipped feature
reversibility_scoring.recommendation: Decision is reversible until Sprint 11 with moderate effort. After Sprint 11, reverting requires deprecating a shipped feature — high cost. Implement kill switch before Sprint 10.

recommended_decision.recommendation: Accelerate PRD pipeline to Sprint 10-11 (Option A). Maintain competitive differentiation lead. Accept medium risk. Implement: 1-week capacity buffer, competitive monitoring escalation trigger, beta testing starting Sprint 9.
assumptions: Engineering team capacity is at 95-100% (not over-allocated)
CompetitorAlpha release cadence remains quarterly (not accelerating)
Gold standard validation can be completed in parallel within 1 sprint
recommended_decision.review_trigger: Revisit this decision if: Sprint 9 beta feedback scores <4/5, OR competitor announces v2 timeline within 4 weeks, OR engineering velocity drops >15% for 2 consecutive sprints
